settings.heading: What materials do you use?
settings.row_content: We use vintage kimonos and obis sourced from Japan, along with reclaimed fabrics and sustainable materials.

settings.heading: How do I care for my garment?
settings.row_content: We recommend spot cleaning, hand washing cold, and air drying. See our Garment Care page for full details.

settings.heading: Do you offer custom orders?
settings.row_content: Yes! Visit our Made to Order collection or Bridal page for custom options.

settings.heading: What are your shipping options?
settings.row_content: Please see our Shipping, Returns & Repairs page for details.

settings.heading: Can I send in my own kimono?
settings.row_content: Absolutely. Send us your heirloom kimono and we'll transform it into a Zatsuyo Jacket made just for you. See our Size Chart for details.

settings.heading: What sizes do you offer?
settings.row_content: Our Zatsuyo Jackets come in three sizes. Check our Size Guide for detailed measurements and fit photos.

settings.heading: How long does a custom order take?
settings.row_content: Custom orders typically take 4-8 weeks depending on complexity. Email us at hello@isaboko.com for a timeline estimate.

settings.heading: What is Patch Club?
settings.row_content: Patch Club is a monthly subscription — 5 patches mailed to you once a month, handmade from vintage kimonos. Visit Patch Club to learn more.
